response.justification: Bogdan has strong individual-contributor credentials spanning 15+ years with genuine developer-tooling exposure (CI/CD setup, test frameworks like Fluent-Asserts and Trial, MCP infrastructure), which partially satisfies the technical side of the role. However, the job is fundamentally a people-leadership position requiring at minimum 2 years of people management, and the resume shows no evidence of managing direct reports at any point in his career. The 'Staff' titles and the GISCollective founder role do not demonstrate people management responsibility. Without this foundational requirement, advancing him would require the hiring team to take a significant risk on an unproven first-time manager for a mid-to-senior management opening.
strengths: 15+ years of software development across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency systems, comfortably exceeding the 8-year minimum
Hands-on developer-tooling background (CI server setup, test runner frameworks, MCP code generators) that maps to the engineering-productivity focus of the team
Track record of knowledge-sharing through workshops, talks, and open-source projects suggests the coaching and mentorship instincts a manager needs
concerns: No evidence of people management or team leadership over direct reports at any employer — the single hardest gap to waive for a people-management role
No experience leading organizational change or setting team-level technical roadmaps; all roles appear to be individual-contributor, however senior
Mobile-platform depth is shallow (one brief iOS stint at WatchUp in 2015–2016), which is a notable weakness given the role's mobile-ecosystem focus

key_factors.factor: Developer tooling and engineering productivity alignment
No organizational change or roadmap leadership
